evals.prompt: Write a subject line and preview text for a marketing email we're sending to announce a new feature — an AI writing assistant built into our product. We want it to feel exciting but not spammy. Our audience is mostly indie hackers and early-stage founders.
evals.expected_output: 3-5 subject line options that stay under 60 characters, avoid spam trigger words, use a direct or curiosity-driven tone appropriate for an indie hacker audience. Preview text written as a continuation of the subject, not a repeat. Brief explanation of the choices made.

evals.prompt: I'm reviewing this HTML email template before we deploy it and I'm worried it might be hurting our deliverability. What should I look for? The template is pretty image-heavy and has a lot of links to our product pages.
evals.expected_output: A structured content audit checklist covering: image-to-text ratio (60% text minimum), alt text on all images, plain-text version presence, link domain reputation, no URL shorteners, inline CSS for compatibility, total email size under 102KB, no JavaScript, CTA button text quality. Should flag the image-heavy pattern as a known spam risk.

evals.prompt: Why does my email footer matter for deliverability? What should always be in it?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of CAN-SPAM footer requirements (physical address, unsubscribe link, company name), why these affect deliverability (complaint rates, legal compliance that inbox providers monitor), and best practice additions like the reason-for-receiving line. Should connect compliance requirements to deliverability outcomes.
